data.title: Visa Expands Stablecoin Settlement in CEMEA Region
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, Visa is broadening its use of stablecoins for transaction settlements in Central and Eastern Europe, the Middle East, and Africa (CEMEA) through a collaboration with crypto infrastructure firm Aquanow. Announced on Thursday, the partnership aims to utilize approved stablecoins like USDC to streamline transactions by reducing costs, operational friction, and settlement times. This initiative responds to the growing demand from banks and payment firms for more efficient cross-border payment solutions. By leveraging stablecoins, Visa intends to digitize the backend of money movement, enabling 24/7 settlement capabilities. Godfrey Sullivan, Visa’s head of product and solutions for the CEMEA region, emphasized that this integration will allow institutions in the area to benefit from faster and simpler settlements. He stated that the partnership with Aquanow is a significant step in modernizing payment systems, reducing dependency on traditional methods with multiple intermediaries, and preparing institutions for the future of financial transactions.

Stablecoins, initially used by cryptocurrency users for transferring funds between exchanges, have evolved to play a role similar to the US dollar in the on-chain economy. Their use is expanding beyond the crypto-native world, now encompassing institutional settlements and payments. In a related development, Deutsche Börse, a market infrastructure provider, announced plans to integrate the EURAU euro-pegged stablecoin issued by AllUnity. This move is part of the exchange group's broader digital-asset strategy, which includes previous collaborations with Circle’s Euro Coin (EURC) and Societe Generale-Forge’s EUR CoinVertible (EURCV). Deutsche Börse plans to incorporate EURAU into its institutional custody service and aims for future integration of the euro stablecoin across its entire service portfolio.

Regulatory discussions continue regarding the classification and supervision of stablecoin exposure within the banking system. Erik Thedéen, governor of the Swedish central bank and chair of the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision, acknowledged the potential need for a revised approach to the current 1,250% risk weighting for crypto exposures. Meanwhile, Bank of England Deputy Governor Sarah Breeden indicated that the UK intends to align with the United States on stablecoin regulation, suggesting that major jurisdictions may proceed in tandem as stablecoins become more integrated into payment and settlement systems.

data.title: Binance will launch AT Earn, Buy, Instant Exchange and Margin services.
data.description: According to the official announcement, Binance will launch the APRO (AT) earning service on November 27, 2025 at 14:00 (UTC). This includes the listing and subscription of AT flexible products on Simple Earn; within one hour of AT spot listing, buy and instant exchange services will be available, supporting zero-fee trading of AT with BTC, USDT, etc.; and at 14:00 (UTC) on the same day, AT will be added as a borrowable asset in the margin service, and AT/USDT and AT/USDC trading pairs will be opened. (This news item was generated with AI assistance)

data.title: Alliance DAO co-founder warns crypto users trading US stocks: You think you're a shark, but you're just a fish.
data.description: The co-founder of Alliance DAO responded to previous comments about crypto brokers facing stronger competition when trading US stocks, stating that he was certain of this because he himself was a "professional counterparty" in the US stock market a decade ago. He said, "About one out of every 20 trades you made in the US stock market was against me. I knew my own level back then, and I knew the strength of peers like Jump and Citadel—we had completely squeezed out any market inefficiencies you could think of." He emphasized that retail investors might "get lucky for a few months," but the only way to make consistent, long-term profits is to buy quality assets and hold them long-term, most likely index funds. If everyone did this, the professional trading industry would not be able to survive. He concluded by writing, "The worst performers at the poker table are always the fish who think they are sharks."

data.title: Binance Blockchain Week 2025: Insights and Innovations in Dubai
data.description:
Binance Blog published a new article, highlighting the upcoming Binance Blockchain Week 2025, set to take place in Dubai. This event aims to provide two days of comprehensive insights into market trends, policy developments, and technological advancements, featuring global leaders who are shaping the future of digital assets. The program is designed around real-world adoption, with sessions covering a wide range of topics including Bitcoin, stablecoins, decentralized finance (DeFi), regulation, Web3 infrastructure, payments, and next-generation products.

Thousands of attendees are expected to gather at Dubai's Coca-Cola Arena, alongside major partners such as Celo, Nexpace, Solayer, and Mastercard, for what promises to be an action-packed crypto event. The full agenda for Binance Blockchain Week Dubai 2025 is now available, offering a detailed look at the ideas, debates, and progress that will be showcased. Participants will have the opportunity to hear from global leaders, breakthrough builders, and industry voices who are influencing the future of digital assets.

Day One of the event will feature Binance CEO Richard Teng, who will discuss the direction of digital assets and the UAE's growing role in global innovation. H.E. Omar Sultan Al Olama, the UAE’s Minister of State for Artificial Intelligence, Digital Economy, and Remote Work Applications, will also speak on how current digital foundations will impact future competitiveness. Michael Saylor, Founder & Executive Chairman of Strategy, will deliver a keynote titled "The Undeniable Case for Bitcoin," followed by a live community AMA session.

Institutional adoption will be a focal point, with leaders from BlackRock, Citi, Franklin Templeton, Julius Baer, and Binance Institutional discussing the evolution of client portfolios and the integration of digital assets into traditional finance. The day will also include updates on next-generation digital-asset technology, featuring BNB Chain and its ecosystem growth. Additionally, discussions will cover stablecoins, compliance frameworks, and U.S. regulatory perspectives, with insights from Tether co-founder Reeve Collins and Payal Patel from Circle.

Day Two will delve into market dynamics, with Raoul Pal from Real Vision providing macro context for 2026's market environment. Discussions will explore on-chain market evolution, seamless digital infrastructure, and the convergence of payments, stable-value assets, and liquidity. AI-led trading insights will be shared, followed by practical discussions on payment rails with Mastercard, Ripple, and TON. The day will also feature a debate on Bitcoin versus tokenized gold, and a showcase of early-stage builders from YZi Labs.

Running parallel to the Main Stage, the Innovation Stage will offer a closer look at emerging tools and technologies. Attendees can expect practical education, infrastructure insights, gaming and AI innovations, and deep dives into next-gen blockchains. The event promises to be a platform where emerging ideas translate into real applications, providing attendees with a comprehensive understanding of the future of digital assets.

data.title: Binance Academy Launches Free Course on Off-Chain Computing
data.description:
Binance Blog published a new article, introducing Binance Academy's latest educational initiative in collaboration with the Marlin Foundation. This free curriculum is designed for Web2 and Web3 developers, focusing on integrating existing backends into the blockchain using off-chain computing units within a Trusted Execution Environment (TEE). The course aims to mentor developers of all experience levels, showcasing practical applications such as an AI-based job-matching app on the BNB Chain. Through these educational efforts, Binance Academy seeks to empower the next generation of decentralized computing innovators.

The course, titled 'Off-chain Computing Using TEE Coprocessors,' is led by Souvik Mishra, Engineering Lead at Maris. It provides a comprehensive guide to building scalable, production-ready systems in decentralized environments. Starting with a theoretical foundation, the course addresses the limitations of on-chain computation and the necessity of off-chain solutions like rollups and coprocessors. It explains how TEEs enable secure, trust-minimized computation, offering both flexibility and strong security guarantees. Participants engage in practical, interactive modules featuring real deployments of AI and DeFi applications powered by TEE coprocessors.

Accessible to the public at no cost via Binance Academy, the course is recommended for aspiring tech professionals of all levels. Module topics include Types of Coprocessors, Virtual Machines vs. Serverless Computing, Key Management Systems, NodeJS servers, and Serverless Subscriptions. The course is designed to develop and strengthen key skills in off-chain computing, secure execution, and decentralized application development.

The launch of this course comes at a crucial time as the demand for decentralized applications (DApps) grows, highlighting the limitations of on-chain computing in terms of scalability and privacy. Industry giants like Google, Apple, and Microsoft have already integrated TEEs and confidential computing as standards for protecting sensitive user data and proprietary AI models within their cloud infrastructure. Binance Academy and Marlin are now leading efforts to bring these standards to the decentralized web.

The Marlin Foundation, an open Web3 infrastructure project focused on high-performance networking and decentralized computing, supports this initiative. With $3 million in seed round financing from Binance Labs in 2019, Marlin aims to provide developers with the tools needed to build performant, production-ready decentralized applications. This partnership reflects Binance and Marlin’s shared mission to empower developers with the tools necessary for creating the next generation of trust-minimized, production-grade decentralized applications.

data.title: Bitcoin Price Could Rally Toward $93K As Bulls Look To Bitcoin Hyper Presale
data.description:
What to Know: Bitcoin trading in a tight range below resistance often pushes traders toward higher‑beta assets that still track the underlying BTC macro trend. Infrastructure that makes Bitcoin more usable for payments, DeFi, and smart contracts is increasingly seen as a leveraged way to express long‑term BTC conviction. Bitcoin Hyper integrates an SVM‑powered Layer 2 with Bitcoin settlement, targeting low‑latency smart contracts to tackle BTC’s speed and programmability limits. Range-bound markets with upside potential toward levels like $93,000 can create strong narratives for BTC-aligned scaling plays and yield-bearing ecosystems
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/news/bitcoin-hyper-presale-as-bitcoin-eyes-93k-range-break/

data.title: Grayscale’s Zcash ETF Bid Triggers ‘GBTC 2.0’ Debate: Crash Or Pump Ahead?
data.description:
Delphi Digital researcher Simon Shockey is arguing that the real story in Zcash is no longer just its price – despite ZEC having one of the most eye-popping rallies of this cycle in recent months. “The most interesting thing about ZEC today is not the price,” he wrote on X. “It’s the fact that a GBTC-style discount dislocation just appeared around ZCSH. ” For Shockey, the Zcash trust setup only makes sense when viewed through the lens of what happened with Grayscale’s Bitcoin product
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/news/grayscales-zcash-etf-crash-or-pump-ahead/

data.title: In the past 24 hours, CEX saw a net outflow of 3958.9 BTC.

The top three CEXs with the largest outflows were: Binance (3,248.53 BTC), OKX (1,038.57 BTC), and Gemini (512.68 BTC). Bitfinex, on the other hand, saw an inflow of 717.97 BTC, ranking first in terms of inflows.

data.title: ECB Maintains Inflation Outlook Amid Uncertainty
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the European Central Bank's meeting minutes from November 27 indicate that its assessment of the inflation outlook remains largely unchanged. Some officials believe that the cycle of interest rate cuts has concluded, as the favorable outlook is likely to persist unless risks materialize.

The central bank suggests that adopting a prudent strategy could enhance the chances of maintaining a positive state. Strategically, monetary policy should not be adjusted for moderate and temporary inflation fluctuations around the target but should only be altered if there is a significant deviation from the target expected in the medium term. However, most members acknowledge that the risks surrounding the inflation outlook are two-sided, and the inflation outlook remains more uncertain than before. Overall, the value of waiting for more information remains high.

data.title: Wall Street Institutions Forecast Continued Growth for U.S. Stocks by 2026
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, several Wall Street institutions have released their forecasts for the U.S. stock market in 2026, suggesting further growth potential. The ongoing AI boom is expected to continue reshaping the economy and financial markets.

Deutsche Bank has set a year-end target of 8,000 points for the S&P 500 index in 2026. HSBC has projected a target of 7,500 points for the same year. Morgan Stanley also anticipates a strong year ahead, predicting the index will close at 7,800 points in 2026. The firm's strategist, Mike Wilson, referred to this as a "new bull market," noting in a report last week that the rolling recession ended earlier this year, with policy support and earnings strength expected to persist into next year.

JPMorgan shares a similar outlook, forecasting a benchmark target of 7,500 points for 2026. However, the bank believes that if inflation prospects improve, prompting the Federal Reserve to adopt a more aggressive rate-cutting approach, the index could surpass 8,000 points. Currently, JPMorgan expects the Federal Reserve to implement two more rate cuts before pausing.

data.title: The Gate contract points system has been online for 30 periods, with a cumulative single account earnings exceeding $1,300.
data.description: Official data shows that since the launch of Gate's pioneering contract points system, it has completed 30 airdrop events, covering over 30 popular cryptocurrencies. The cumulative earnings per account have exceeded $1300, with the highest single-event airdrop yielding over $127. The performance is impressive. It is understood that Gate's contract points mechanism is the first of its kind in the industry, creating a virtuous cycle between trading incentives and community rewards, bringing continuous high returns to active users.
